decks.name: Healthy Food
decks.description: Learn about nutritious foods and why they are important for our body

cards.front: Why do we need to eat food?
cards.back:
We need food to get energy, to grow, to repair our body, and to stay healthy.

Food gives us the strength to study, play, and do all our daily activities.
cards.hint: Think about what happens when you skip a meal

cards.back:
A balanced diet is a meal that has the right amount of all the nutrients our body needs.

It includes cereals, pulses, vegetables, fruits, milk, and a little oil and sugar.
cards.hint: Think of a thali with different types of food

cards.front: Why should we eat fruits and vegetables every day?
cards.back:
Fruits and vegetables give us vitamins and minerals that protect us from diseases.

They also have fibre that helps our stomach digest food properly.

Water helps our body digest food, carry nutrients, remove waste, and keep our body cool.

We should drink 6-8 glasses of clean water every day.
cards.hint: Our body is mostly made up of this

cards.front: What happens if we eat too much junk food?
cards.back:
Eating too much junk food can make us unhealthy. It can cause tooth decay, weight gain, tiredness, and weakness.

Junk food has lots of oil, sugar, and salt but very few nutrients.
cards.hint: Think of chips, samosa, and cold drinks

decks.name: Food Groups
decks.description: Understand the different groups of food and what they do for our body

cards.front: What are energy-giving foods? Give examples.
cards.back:
Energy-giving foods are rich in carbohydrates and fats. They give us energy to work, play, and study.

Examples: rice, roti, bread, potatoes, ghee, butter, and oil.
cards.hint: Foods that give us the power to run and play

cards.front: What are body-building foods? Give examples.
cards.back:
Body-building foods are rich in proteins. They help our body grow and repair damaged parts.

Examples: dal, paneer, milk, eggs, fish, soybean, and nuts.
cards.hint: Foods that help us grow taller and stronger

cards.front: What are protective foods? Give examples.
cards.back:
Protective foods are rich in vitamins and minerals. They protect us from getting sick.

Examples: green leafy vegetables, carrots, oranges, amla, papaya, and tomatoes.
cards.hint: Foods that act like a shield against diseases

cards.front: Name two foods that are commonly eaten together in India to make a complete meal.
cards.back:
Dal and rice (or dal and roti) are commonly eaten together.

Dal gives us proteins (body-building) and rice gives us carbohydrates (energy). Together they make a nutritious meal!
cards.hint: A very popular combination in Indian households

cards.front: Why do different people eat different kinds of food?
cards.back:
Food habits depend on where people live, what grows in their area, their culture, religion, and family traditions.

For example, people in South India eat more rice, while people in North India eat more roti.
cards.hint: Think about what your family eats compared to friends from other states
